ruleset.name: Design
ruleset.description: Rules that help you discover design issues.

rule.name: CollapsibleIfStatements
rule.message: This if statement could be combined with its parent using &&

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

property.name: xpath
property.value: (: No-braces outer body: if (a) if (b) { ... } :) //IfExpression[not(T-ELSE)] /ControlStructureBody[not(Block)] /Statement/Expression//PrimaryExpression /IfExpression[not(T-ELSE) and not(ancestor::CallSuffix)] | (: Braces outer body with a single inner statement :) //IfExpression[not(T-ELSE)] /ControlStructureBody/Block[count(Statements/Statement) = 1] /Statements/Statement/Expression//PrimaryExpression /IfExpression[not(T-ELSE) and not(ancestor::CallSuffix)]

rule.name: SimplifyBooleanExpressions
rule.message: Avoid unnecessary comparisons in boolean expressions
rule.description:
Avoid redundant boolean identity expressions using `&&` or `||` with a literal: `x && true` and `x || false` are no-ops and can be simplified to just `x`.
Note: `x == true` and similar equality comparisons are intentionally not flagged -- in Kotlin these are idiomatic for nullable `Boolean?` values.
Kotlin equivalent of Java rule: category/java/design.xml/SimplifyBooleanExpressions

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: SimplifyBooleanReturns
rule.message: This if/else expression can be simplified by using the condition directly

property.name: javaEquivalentRule
property.description: Java rule this Kotlin rule is based on; update this rule when the Java rule changes.

property.name: xpath

rule.name: ExcessiveParameterList
rule.message: Avoid long parameter lists ({0} parameters - threshold is {1}).
rule.description: Functions and constructors with numerous parameters are a challenge to maintain, especially if most of them share the same datatype. These situations usually denote the need for new data classes to wrap the numerous parameters.
